item.title: Sorry for editing I thought it was my sandbox
item.content:encoded: Sorry for editing this I thought it was my sandbox all I did was edit the info at the top but I fixed it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After reading this for the third time, I can confirm that this is a fantastic read, that I absolutely enjoyed thoroughly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll be doing some editing on this in my sandbox. I appreciate everybody's votes, I'm very glad the majority liked the article. As per the criticisms, I agree with Roget and ModernMajorGeneral on the majority of points.

The exploration log serves as a foreshadowing device. I will look into minimizing meaningless and filler information though I would like to maintain some sort of foreshadowing point here.

Dr. Joy's name actually wasn't on purpose and I hadn't noticed the coincidence there. The original inspiration for the name was Nurse Joy from the Pokemon series (don't ask why, it just popped in my head). However, I will be leaving the name as-is.

Regarding the tangent on sertraline, I agree that it is an unneeded tangent. I wanted to cover the "why didn't they try antidepressants" part of the therapy. I will cut back on this tangent and perhaps muddle it down to a single-line reference.

I will also be editing a lot of dialogue as that is my weakest trait in my opinion.

The header was removed. Thank you!

Edit: I have added the above changes. Thank you, everyone, for the feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreeing with Roget. This was promising at the start - a simple but intriguing base idea supported by some very solid pictures. It started coming apart after the first interview log, though.

The first exploration log didn't add anything - it didn't say anything interesting about any of the MTF characters (and even if it had killing them off immediately would have rendered it meaningless) or reveal any new information that couldn't have been (and mostly was) explained in half a paragraph of description about the town.

Naming a therapist "Dr Joy" is too much. This is one of those situations where it might happen in reality - there are some people in real life named Joy and some of those people are probably therapists, and I've met some health workers with names that sound like jokes - but in a fictional work it sounds like a bad joke.

I don't see the point of a tangent about antidepressants and blood tests. If you wanted to show that the therapist really did care about him to the extent of carefully considering all treatment options, a single line of "antidepressants were trialled with minimal effect" would have got it out of the way without needing an addendum about how he picked up a medication cup and took a blood sample and so on.

There are some bits here which are questionable depictions of mental illness - particularly the first log, where he seems to be on the way to being cured by having a karaoke session, which is… problematic… - and lines such as "SCP-5027 continues to show symptoms of severe depression, PTSD, and anxiety. At this time, these symptoms are considered to be standard personality traits of SCP-5027." (PTSD isn't a "personality trait", these are mutually exclusive diagnoses), or the whole sertraline business (50mg is a normal starting dose and blood levels are rarely done). None of the mental illness stuff is particularly egregious on its own, but is noticeable when the whole point of the article is mental health.

The main problem with the article was as Roget said, though - it's another 'sad humanoid in containment' story, albeit one with an above-average amount of effort put into it, and the beats of the story are interchangeable with most of the other sad humanoids in containment. The character of the therapist also stretches plausibility - the final section where she just gets drunk, unloads all her personal problems on him, breaches all therapist boundaries (and SCP containment procedures) and predictably dies somehow managed to come totally out of nowhere yet be completely expected for this sort of sad humanoid article.

Also the animated header… just why? The rotating animated logo is distracting and adds nothing of interest. It's another thing that would have been better off trimmed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I think if anything Williams would be reprimanded for interfering with the therapist's ability to perform her duties and in the process making a skip more difficult to contain.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just cannot buy this sort of thing happening. Everything the foundation does is to *prevent* breaches; SCP designations are used in documentation to prevent ordinary personnel from getting attached, but Psychologists and etc get passes because they are qualified to interact with the SCPs in this manner. Like, it is so blindingly apparent that if they just let the shrink do their job, *none of this would have happened*.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A promising humanoid SCP article let down by interview logs that substitute curse words for character. The effect here has a lot of potential for pathos but frankly he reads as the exact same character I could probably pick out from half a dozen other SCPs. The last log in particular just doesn't work at all, it's as far as you can get from the experience of talking to someone who's just tried to commit suicide on both ends of the conversation(at least as far as my personal experience goes.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like how you showed the foundation being emotionless and clinical while having dr joy express humanity towards 5027. It shows how the foundation is like but also that you can’t take away the instinctive compassion humans have to each other. Haven’t really seen other SCPs that I can remember do that. A good SCP over all ;)
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think it is really well written and shows the personality and relationships of the characters very well :)
You did good here!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After lots of crit, rewrites, and chopping useless info, I finally present Schrodinger's Man. Thanks to Sonderance, Cyvstvi13, and DrUnorignal for the exceptional feedback and helping me make this long article easy to digest. Please let me know what you think! Thanks.

For those wondering why the name remains redacted:

+ show block
– hide block

It's implied that Dr. Williams redacted it after Dr. Joys' death under the logic that it's hard to form a connection to something without a name.

Images from Creative Commons.
https://live.staticflickr.com/2600/4148164770_c8185a0367_b.jpg
https://live.staticflickr.com/5806/22987710600_34dea6ba7e_b.jpg
https://live.staticflickr.com/2855/11958908843_40e59087bc_b.jpg
